text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-on bounced, but tech still led the tape. The day was a rotation back into beaten-down growth and semis, while broader breadth stayed constructive with small caps and equal-weighted exposure outperforming large-cap benchmarks[1][3]. The index mix says dip-buying returned, but not enough to fully repair the prior tech unwind, and oil’s easing helped risk sentiment even as rate/fear sensitivity stayed in the background[1][3].

INDICES — VIX was **19.9**, up on the day and above its 20-day average of 17.2, so fear stayed elevated[4]. Best-to-worst: IJR +0.95%, IJH +0.84%, RSP +0.76%, IWM +0.32%, DIA +0.10%, SPY -0.29%, QQQ -1.15%, SOXX -1.63%; **Small > large by 1.24pp**[4]. The most notable cross-asset move was **USO -2.85%**, which relieved pressure on risk assets[4].

VOLUME — SPY vol_ratio was **3.1x** on -0.26%, which reads as **distribution**; QQQ vol_ratio was **2.3x** with semis and tech gaining attention, but the index print still looked mixed versus the headline tape[5]. SOXX at **2.5x** and IJH at **2.0x** show money concentrating in chips and mid-caps, while IWM **1.4x** and DIA **1.3x** suggest the rest of the tape was comparatively ignored[5]. On the sector side, XLK **2.1x** confirmed active rotation into tech, while XLU **0.9x** and XLY **1.1x** looked low-conviction[5].

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLRE Real Estate +2.13%, XLB Materials +1.62%, XLV Healthcare +1.26%, XLP Staples +1.24%, XLI Industrials +1.13%, XLU Utilities +1.06%, XLF Financials +0.94%, XLY Discretionary +0.42%, XLC Comms +0.35%, XLE Energy -1.61%, XLK Technology -1.85%. Top industries were XHB +3.61% and JETS +3.10%; weakest were MSOS -4.52% and SLV -4.17%[4]. Money moved into real estate, materials, and healthcare while money came out of energy and tech; that is *defensive rotation with selective growth pressure*, not a clean risk-on breakout[4]. Rotation-state: **rangebound chop**[4].

POSITIONING — SPY and QQQ both showed elevated put demand with PCR_vol of **1.60** and **1.46**, and both carried positive skew at **+12.2%** and **+14.3%**, so protection demand stayed firm. VIX at **19.9** sat above its 20-day average of **17.2**, confirming elevated fear rather than complacency[4]. IWM’s PCR_vol of **2.87** plus gamma flip at **255.0** points to more fragile small-cap positioning.

CROSS-ASSET — Oil’s **-2.85%** drop and gold’s **-1.63%** drop both lowered the inflation/fear bid, while equities still failed to sustain leadership in QQQ[4]. That combination says the market was trading *relief on geopolitics* more than a full macro risk repricing[1][4].

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed **68/100 Greed**; top movers were SLV **-4.17%** in metals, MSOS **-4.52%** in cannabis, and JETS **+3.10%** in transports[4]. 20d momentum extremes: best sector was XLV **+5.98%**, worst sector was XLC **-3.78%**; best basket was SOXX **+5.51%**, worst basket was SLV **-24.35%**[4]. DRIV **2.8x** in industries and DBC **2.7x** showed attention in cyclicals/commodities, but both finished weaker, which reads as distribution[5]. Watch QQQ **-1.15%** for follow-through after elevated vol, SPY **-0.29%** for distribution versus support, and IWM **+0.32%** near a fragile gamma setup[4].
